title: Quiz: Linear Independence
description: Test your understanding of linear independence, span, basis, and dimension

questions.question: Which of the following sets of vectors is linearly independent?
questions.explanation: a) Dependent: [2,4]ᵀ = 2×[1,2]ᵀ. b) Dependent: 3 vectors in 2D space. c) Independent: standard basis vectors. d) Dependent: contains zero vector.

questions.question: What is the dimension of the span of vectors {[1,0,0]ᵀ, [0,1,0]ᵀ, [1,1,0]ᵀ}?
questions.explanation: The third vector [1,1,0]ᵀ = [1,0,0]ᵀ + [0,1,0]ᵀ, so it's redundant. The span is the xy-plane, which is 2-dimensional.

questions.question: What is the dimension of the state space for 3 qubits?
questions.explanation: For n qubits, the dimension is 2ⁿ. For 3 qubits: 2³ = 8. The basis states are |000⟩, |001⟩, |010⟩, |011⟩, |100⟩, |101⟩, |110⟩, |111⟩.

questions.question: If a matrix A has rank 3 and 5 columns, what can you conclude about the column vectors?

options.text: A maximum of 3 vectors can be linearly independent

questions.explanation: Rank equals the maximum number of linearly independent columns. With rank 3, at most 3 columns are independent, and the 5 columns span a 3-dimensional space.

questions.question: The set {|0⟩, |1⟩} and the set {|+⟩, |-⟩} both form valid bases for the single-qubit state space.
questions.explanation: True! Both sets contain 2 linearly independent vectors that span ℂ². Any qubit state can be written as a linear combination of either basis.

questions.question: If {v₁, v₂, v₃} is linearly dependent, then {v₁, v₂} must also be linearly dependent.
questions.explanation: False! For example, {[1,0,0], [0,1,0], [1,1,0]} is dependent (third is sum of first two), but {[1,0,0], [0,1,0]} is independent.

questions.question: What is span{|0⟩ + |1⟩}?
questions.explanation: The span of a single non-zero vector is a 1-dimensional subspace - all scalar multiples c(|0⟩ + |1⟩) for c ∈ ℂ. This is a line through the origin.

questions.question: Which of the following are true about a basis? (Select all that apply)

options.text: The vectors must be linearly independent
The vectors must span the space
There is exactly one basis for each vector space
All bases of a space have the same number of vectors

questions.explanation: a) ✓ Linear independence is required. b) ✓ Spanning is required. c) ✗ There are infinitely many bases! d) ✓ All bases have the same size (the dimension).
